description:
You are a localization engineer working on a small French-language update.  
The project files are already on disk:

1. /home/user/project/localization/translations_master.tsv – a **TAB-separated** table with four columns in this order:  
   key ⟶ en_US ⟶ fr_FR ⟶ es_ES

2. /home/user/project/localization/fr_updates.tsv – a **TAB-separated** one-column file that contains only the *new* French strings.  
   It has the same row order as translations_master.tsv (there is one header line followed by five data lines).

Goal
Create a new file called  
/home/user/project/localization/fr_release.tsv  
that contains **exactly two columns, TAB-separated, in this precise order**:

• Column 1: key (copied from translations_master.tsv)  
• Column 2: the updated French strings (taken from fr_updates.tsv)

The resulting fr_release.tsv must contain exactly 6 lines (1 header + 5 data rows) and every line must end with a single newline (LF, “\n”).  
The header line must be:

key<TAB>fr_FR

Under that header the rows must appear in the same order as they do in translations_master.tsv.

Constraints

• Use standard command-line tools only; the intent is to solve the task with a *simple sequence of cut and paste (or similar) commands* – no manual editing or spreadsheet software.  
• Do not alter the source files.  
• Do not reorder, add, or remove rows.  
• The delimiter is always a single horizontal TAB character (ASCII 0x09).  
• Ensure the final file’s name and directory path are exact so that the automated checker can locate it.

Hints

1. One typical approach is to
   a) extract the first column (“key”) from translations_master.tsv with cut,  
   b) align it side-by-side with fr_updates.tsv using paste,  
   c) redirect the result to fr_release.tsv.
2. Verify the header of fr_release.tsv – it must be exactly "key<TAB>fr_FR".

When you are done, the automated grader will open /home/user/project/localization/fr_release.tsv and compare it byte-for-byte against the expected answer.
truth:
<!--
Pre-existing files and their exact contents (each line ends with '\n').
TAB characters are represented here as \t for readability; real files contain actual ASCII 0x09 bytes.
-->

File: /home/user/project/localization/translations_master.tsv
Contents:
key\ten_US\tfr_FR\tes_ES\n
greeting\tHello\tBonjour\tHola\n
farewell\tGoodbye\tAu revoir\tAdiós\n
thanks\tThank you\tMerci\tGracias\n
yes\tYes\tOui\tSí\n
no\tNo\tNon\tNo\n

File: /home/user/project/localization/fr_updates.tsv
Contents:
fr_FR\n
Salut\n
Au revoir\n
Merci\n
Oui\n
Non\n

File that must be produced by the agent: /home/user/project/localization/fr_release.tsv
Expected contents:
key\tfr_FR\n
greeting\tSalut\n
farewell\tAu revoir\n
thanks\tMerci\n
yes\tOui\n
no\tNon\n
name: task_000000_c4fa82ae
